factors.summary: The company is strengthening its balance sheet through deleveraging and refinancing.

evidence.text: We expect our ongoing sources of liquidity to include cash generated from operations, borrowings under our revolving credit facility and Receivables Facility, and the issuance of equity and/or debt securities as appropriate given market conditions.

factors.summary: The cash flow analysis shows a significant decrease in net cash provided by operating activities and an increase in net cash used in financing activities.

evidence.text: For the Six Months Ended (in thousands) June 30, 2020 June 30, 2019 $ Change Net cash provided by operating activities $ 629,007 $ 979,172 $ (350,165) Net cash used in investing activities $ (535,574) $ (787,639) $ 252,065 Net cash used in financing activities $ (96,027) $ (515,435) $ 419,408

factors.summary: There have been no material changes to commitments and contractual obligations except for changes to long-term debt.

evidence.text: In our 2019 Annual Report, we disclosed our commitments and contractual obligations. There have been no material changes to these commitments and contractual obligations except for the changes to our long-term debt during the six months ended June 30, 2020, as described above.

factors.summary: There have been no material changes to off-balance sheet arrangements, and critical accounting policies are based on estimates and judgments.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to our off-balance sheet arrangements as disclosed in our 2019 Annual Report during the six months ended June 30, 2020. The accompanying condensed consolidated financial statements are prepared in accordance with GAAP, which requires us to select accounting policies and make estimates that affect amounts reported in the condensed consolidated financial statements and the accompanying notes. Management’s estimates are based on the relevant information available at the end of each period.
evidence.subsection: OFF-BALANCE SHEET ARRANGEMENTS / CRITICAL ACCOUNTING POLICIES AND ESTIMATES

factors.summary: The document includes cautionary statements regarding forward-looking information and the risks associated with such statements.

evidence.text: This Quarterly Report contains certain information that may constitute “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. [...] Without limiting the generality of the preceding sentence, any time we use the words “expects,” “intends,” “will,” “anticipates,” “believes,” “confident,” “continue,” “propose,” “seeks,” “could,” “may,” “should,” “estimates,” “forecasts,” “might,” “goals,” “objectives,” “targets,” “planned,” “projects,” and similar expressions, we intend to clearly express that the information deals with possible future events and is forward-looking in nature. [...] Therefore, caution should be taken not to place undue reliance on any such forward-looking statements.

factors.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic is creating a challenging economic environment which is acting as a headwind for the company.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 Pandemic could have a significant negative impact on our employees, our customers, our suppliers and our ability to carry on our normal operations given its impact on the economy generally, as well as the resulting 'shelter in place' and other operational requirements we have or must continue to adhere to, or which could be reinstituted upon a re-emergence of COVID-19 in a particular jurisdiction, any of which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

factors.summary: The company faces potential litigation risks related to a data breach incident.

evidence.text: In April 2020, after investigating a customer inquiry, we disclosed that a Company technician had, during service visits, added his personal email address to 220 of our customers' accounts, which provided this employee with varying levels of unauthorized personal access to such customers' in-home security systems, including, in some cases, video streams, over a period of several years. We immediately terminated this employee. While we have notified all such customers of this activity, and have reached resolution with several of these customers, we cannot be certain that others impacted will not continue to make legal claims against us, either individually or as a class.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly related to privacy and data security laws such as GDPR and CCPA, are creating new compliance obligations for the company.

evidence.text: The General Data Protection Regulation ('GDPR') applies to activities regarding personal data that are conducted by us, directly or indirectly through vendors and subcontractors, from an establishment in the European Union. As interpretation and enforcement of the GDPR evolves, it will create a range of new compliance obligations, which could cause us to change our business practices, with the possibility for significant financial penalties for noncompliance.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin residential services, partially offset by lower commercial revenue.

evidence.text: The increase in installation and other revenue was primarily due to higher volume of revenue from equipment sold outright to residential customers as a result of the Defenders Acquisition and the Equipment Ownership Model Change. These increases were partially offset by a decrease in the volume of revenue from equipment sold outright to commercial customers as a result of the COVID-19 Pandemic and due to the sale of ADT Canada.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident in reduced attrition and lower service costs.

evidence.text: The decrease in attrition was due to an improvement in disconnects primarily from relocations. The decrease in service costs was primarily as a result of the sale of ADT Canada.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Input costs are stable as the company transitions to a Company-owned model.

evidence.text: The company is transitioning back to a predominately Company-owned model for residential transactions, which is expected to normalize cost structures.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with incremental expenses offset by cost reductions.

evidence.text: The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses was partially offset by a reduction in expenses of $18 million due to the sale of ADT Canada as well as a decrease in advertising expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Management expects a deceleration in growth due to the transition to a Company-owned model.

evidence.text: We do not expect to experience the same level of revenue growth subsequent to June 30, 2020 as reported in our results of operations for the three months ended June 30, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing due to increased demand for smart home solutions and DIY products.

evidence.text: Customers’ increasingly mobile and active lifestyles have created new opportunities for us in the fast-growing market for self-monitored DIY products and mobile technology. Our technology also allows us to integrate with various third-party connected and wearable devices so that we can service our customers whether they are at home or on-the-go.

factors.summary: The commercial end market has been more significantly impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic than the residential market.

evidence.text: While the COVID-19 Pandemic has impacted our commercial channel to a greater extent than our residential channel...

factors.summary: Management expresses confidence in the company's financial foundation and ability to maintain operations during the pandemic.

evidence.text: We have not sought or requested government assistance as a result of the COVID-19 Pandemic, but we do expect to recognize favorable cash flows and other benefits associated with certain income tax and payroll tax provisions of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (the 'CARES Act').

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential impact from the COVID-19 pandemic on commercial channels and the transition back to a Company-owned model.

evidence.text: While the COVID-19 Pandemic has impacted our commercial channel to a greater extent than our residential channel... Additionally, we believe the economic downturn... may help counterbalance any increase in gross customer revenue attrition that we may experience... We do not expect to experience the same level of revenue growth subsequent to June 30, 2020 as reported in our results of operations for the three and six months ended June 30, 2020.

factors.summary: The company is extending security concepts to personal and cybersecurity, indicating a clear long-term strategic direction.

evidence.text: As part of our innovative and dynamic growth markets, we are extending the concept of security from the physical home or business to personal on-the-go security and safety and cybersecurity.

factors.summary: Potential catalysts include opportunities for additional acquisitions and investments in new revenue streams.

evidence.text: Finally, we may see opportunities for additional acquisitions, continued investment in potential new revenue streams or capabilities, and low cost bulk account purchases.

factors.summary: The company is integrating with third-party devices and adopting digital technologies to enhance customer service.

evidence.text: Our technology also allows us to integrate with various third-party connected and wearable devices so that we can service our customers whether they are at home or on-the-go.

factors.summary: The company continues to return capital to shareholders through a regular quarterly dividend despite the pandemic.

evidence.text: We have not sought or requested government assistance as a result of the COVID-19 Pandemic, but we do expect to recognize favorable cash flows and other benefits associated with certain income tax and payroll tax provisions of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (the 'CARES Act'). While we have incurred additional costs associated with personal protective equipment for our employees and work from home actions, we also instituted various temporary cost control measures such as certain hiring freezes and voluntary furloughs for employees. Furthermore, we believe the economic downturn, the recent civil unrest, shelter in place requirements, and continued economic and COVID-19 Pandemic uncertainties increase awareness of the need for security, which together with an anticipated lower volume of customer relocations and the utilization of temporary pricing and retention initiatives for existing customers, may help counterbalance any increase in gross customer revenue attrition that we may experience as a result of reduced consumer or business spending caused by the COVID-19 Pandemic. Finally, we may see opportunities for additional acquisitions, continued investment in potential new revenue streams or capabilities, and low cost bulk account purchases.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed, with some stability observed but also facing pressures from increased costs and economic factors.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are stable, with no significant compression or expansion.

The increase in cost of revenue was primarily due to an increase in installation costs associated with a higher volume of transactions in which equipment was sold outright to residential customers as a result of the Defenders Acquisition and the Equipment Ownership Model Change. These increases were partially offset by a decrease in installation costs associated with a lower volume of transactions in which equipment was sold outright to commercial customers as a result of the COVID-19 Pandemic and due to the sale of ADT Canada.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with slight increases noted in recent periods. Despite challenges posed by the pandemic, the company anticipates continued strong cash flow generation, partially supported by benefits from tax provisions under the CARES Act.

evidence.text: Free Cash Flow was $271,068 for the six months ended June 30, 2020, reflecting a slight improvement over the previous period.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We have not sought or requested government assistance as a result of the COVID-19 Pandemic, but we do expect to recognize favorable cash flows and other benefits associated with certain income tax and payroll tax provisions of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (the 'CARES Act').

factors.summary: Demand for ADT's security and automation solutions shows mixed trends, with stable demand for monitoring and related services despite customer attrition and economic factors, but influenced negatively by external factors such as economic conditions, housing market growth, and crime perception.

evidence.text: The decrease in monitoring and related services revenue was driven by a decrease in recurring revenue. Recurring revenue decreased primarily from a reduction of revenue due to the sale of ADT Canada and customer attrition. These decreases were partially offset by improvements in average pricing, which was driven by the addition of new customers at higher rates as compared to the existing customer base.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: The overall economic condition in the geographies in which we operate can impact our ability to attract new customers and grow our business in all customer channels. Growth in our residential customer base can be influenced by the overall state of the housing market. Growth in our commercial customer base can be influenced by the rate at which new businesses begin operations or existing businesses grow. The demand for our products and services is also impacted by the perceived threat of crime, as well as the quality of the service of our competitors.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to decelerate due to a shift back to a Company-owned model post-transition.

evidence.text: The decrease in RMR to $339 million as of June 30, 2020 from $351 million as of June 30, 2019 was primarily due to the sale of ADT Canada, which decreased RMR by approximately $16 million, and customer attrition.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: We do not expect to experience the same level of revenue growth subsequent to June 30, 2020 as reported in our results of operations for the three and six months ended June 30, 2020. We are in the early stages of our new revenue model initiative and we cannot be certain that this initiative or our transition back to a predominately Company-owned model... will achieve the desired outcomes. Accordingly, the results of the new revenue model initiative and impact of our transition back to a predominately Company-owned model could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows, and key performance indicators.

factors.summary: The company is effectively enhancing its pricing power through the addition of new customers who opt for higher-priced services. However, the ability to further increase monthly fees depends on the successful introduction and marketing of additional features and services that provide enhanced customer value.

evidence.text: Improvements in average pricing were driven by the addition of new customers at higher rates as new customers generally selected higher priced services as compared to our existing customer base.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Our ability to increase monthly fees at the individual customer level depends on a number of factors, including our ability to effectively introduce and market additional features and services that increase the value of our offerings to customers, which we believe drives customers to purchase higher levels of service and supports our ability to make periodic adjustments to pricing.

factors.summary: The company has been active in acquisition activity, primarily highlighted by the Defenders Acquisition, which is creating value but also posing challenges. There is potential for further acquisitions and investments in new revenue streams, indicating an ongoing strategic focus on growth through mergers and acquisitions.

evidence.text: The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses was primarily due to $75 million of incremental expenses associated with the Defenders Acquisition...
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Finally, we may see opportunities for additional acquisitions, continued investment in potential new revenue streams or capabilities, and low cost bulk account purchases.

factors.summary: The company implemented cost reduction measures such as hiring freezes and voluntary furloughs in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. However, despite these actions, there is still margin pressure due to increased costs, particularly in installation and service areas, which were partially offset by lower transaction volumes and the sale of ADT Canada.

evidence.text: Cost reduction actions are partially offsetting increased costs, but margin pressure remains.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: The increase in cost of revenue was primarily due to an increase in installation costs... partially offset by a decrease in installation costs associated with a lower volume of transactions in which equipment was sold outright to commercial customers as a result of the COVID-19 Pandemic and due to the sale of ADT Canada, as well as a decrease in service costs primarily as a result of the sale of ADT Canada.

Furthermore, we believe the economic downturn... may help counterbalance any increase in gross customer revenue attrition that we may experience... Furthermore, we believe the economic downturn, the recent civil unrest, shelter in place requirements, and continued economic and COVID-19 Pandemic uncertainties increase awareness of the need for security, which together with an anticipated lower volume of customer relocations and the utilization of temporary pricing and retention initiatives for existing customers, may help counterbalance any increase in gross customer revenue attrition that we may experience as a result of reduced consumer or business spending caused by the COVID-19 Pandemic. Finally, we may see opportunities for additional acquisitions, continued investment in potential new revenue streams or capabilities, and low cost bulk account purchases.
